item.title: Airbnb Boosts 2026 Revenue Outlook on Increased Bookings
item.description: Airbnb Inc. boosted its revenue growth forecast for the year on “particularly strong” bookings in the Americas and benefits from simplifying its fee structure for hosts and guests.

item.title: Expedia Revenue Beat Shows Middle East Resilience, Eyes World Cup
item.description: Expedia Group Inc. posted quarterly revenue that came in slightly ahead of estimates as it overcame Middle East-related travel disruptions and flagged healthy demand from this summer’s FIFA World Cup.

item.title: MercadoLibre Slips As Profits Miss Despite Revenue Surge
item.description: Shares in e-commerce and fintech giant MercadoLibre Inc. slipped after profits missed estimates for the fourth quarter in a row even as revenue grew at its fastest pace in nearly four years.

item.title: IEA Head Calls on Canada to Move Faster on Energy Amid Supply Shock
item.description: The head of the International Energy Agency is urging Canada to move more quickly to develop and export its energy resources, warning that market conditions will only favor the country for so long.

item.title: Latin America’s Largest Economy Sets Export Record on Oil Price Surge
item.description: Brazil set a monthly export record in April as war in the Middle East propelled oil prices higher, according to government data published on Thursday.

item.title: Private Credit’s Birthplace Gives New Rise to 1980s-Style Greed
item.description: Attendees walk past signage of the Milken Institute during the 29th annual Milken Institute Global Conference at the Beverly Hilton in Beverly Hills, California on May 5, 2026.

item.title: US Consumer Borrowing Increased in March by Most Since 2022
item.description: US consumer borrowing increased in March by the most since late 2022, reflecting a surge in non-revolving credit as well as higher credit-card balances.

item.title: Chilean President Kast’s Mega Economic Bill Clears First Hurdle as Trouble Brews
item.description: Chilean President José Antonio Kast’s flagship economic bill cleared its first congressional hurdle on Thursday, but the vote gives the new administration little respite as it works to muster legislative support.

item.title: La informalidad impulsa el reinado del efectivo en México
item.description: La arraigada cultura del pago en efectivo del país se mantiene firme frente a un esfuerzo implacable de cientos de millones de dólares por parte de las empresas de tecnología financiera

item.title: Banxico Cuts Rate to 6.5% in Possible End to Easing Cycle
item.description: Mexico reduced borrowing costs by a quarter of a percentage point, a move that the central bank’s chief signaled could mark the end of the easing cycle that began two years ago.

item.title: Trump’s Summit With Xi Is On Despite China’s Iran Concerns
item.description: President Donald Trump is forging ahead with plans to hold a summit with counterpart Xi Jinping next week in Beijing, despite unease among Chinese officials about holding the high-stakes meeting before the war in Iran is resolved.

item.title: Colombia Buys Dollars in Spot Market as Swap Payment Looms
item.description: Colombia’s finance ministry is once again buying dollars in the spot market, traders say, as authorities gear up to pay off a controversial Swiss franc swap before the presidential election later this month.

item.title: Fed’s Kashkari Says Next Rate Move Uncertain Because of Iran War
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of Minneapolis President Neel Kashkari said the conflict in the Middle East has added uncertainty to the outlook for interest rates.

item.title: Bessent Meets Insurance Regulators to Discuss Private Credit
item.description: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent met with insurance regulators to talk about their exposure to private credit as the increasingly complex ties between the two sectors fuels mounting regulatory scrutiny.

item.title: Apple’s Camera-Equipped AirPods Reach Late Testing in AI Device Push
item.description: Apple Inc. has reached the late stages of development for new AirPods with built-in cameras, a significant milestone for what will likely be its first wearable device designed for the artificial intelligence era.

item.title: Binance Says Treasury Sent Letter Over Monitorship of Exchange
item.description: Binance, which agreed to a monitor while pleading guilty to US charges related to sanctions and anti–money-laundering violations almost three years ago, received a letter from the Department of the Treasury asking for interviews with employees of the crypto exchange and records to investigate potential sanctions violations, according to a person familiar with the matter.

item.title: Everyone Wants a Piece of Ina Garten’s Forthcoming Podcast
item.description: Deal offers are reaching seven figures a year for a program that has yet to launch.

item.title: Honduras Reviews China Ties Amid Push to Court US Investment
item.description: Honduras’ new president is reviewing his predecessor’s agreements with China, a process that could help the US push to reduce the Asian nation’s influence in the region and lead to recognition of Taiwan.

item.title: Cheniere Slumps on Surprise $3.5 Billion Loss on Derivatives
item.description: Cheniere Energy Inc. plunged almost 10% after posting a surprise $3.5 billion loss driven by a drop in the value of hedges as the Iran war upended natural gas markets.

item.title: Apollo’s Marc Rowan Warns of Fallout From ‘Massive Geopolitical’ Shift
item.description: Apollo Global Management Inc. Chief Executive Officer Marc Rowan said the world isn’t prepared for the shakeout from artificial intelligence, price pressures and rising government debt.

item.title: Ecuador Regulators Probe Major Bank in Years-Old Laundering Case
item.description: Financial regulators in Ecuador have launched a joint probe into possible money laundering at Banco Guayaquil, the nation’s third-biggest bank by net profit.

item.title: Quebec Trade Rep Urges Canada to Counter US View It’s Stalling Trade Talks
item.description: Canada needs to act quickly to push back on a growing belief in Washington that it’s dragging its feet on trade talks, according to the Quebec government’s representative in those negotiations.

item.title: Fed’s Daly Says Statement Language Is Less Important Than Action
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco President Mary Daly downplayed division among policymakers over the central bank’s policy statement, signaling she wouldn’t have dissented against it as some of her voting colleagues did.

item.title: Ghana Targets $1 Billion in Cocoa Bonds as Part of Overhaul
item.description: Ghana is planning to raise $1 billion through domestic bonds to fund cocoa purchases from farmers, part of an overhaul of the way it delivers the commodity to global buyers.

item.title: Schnabel Says ECB Will Need to Hike If Energy Shock Broadens
item.description: The European Central Bank will have to raise interest rates if the Iran war leaves a more lasting mark on inflation, according to Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel.

item.title: Mexico Plans $8 Billion in Gas Pipelines to Boost Power Sector
item.description: Mexico is planning to invest 140 billion pesos ($8.1 billion) in new gas pipelines over the next four years, the latest step in President Claudia Sheinbaum’s plan to boost the country’s power generation.

item.title: Organic Juice Maker Suja Life Sinks 14% After $186.7 Million IPO
item.description: Suja Life Inc. shares fell 14% in its trading debut on Thursday, after the initial public offering for the maker of organic cold-pressed juices and other nutritional beverages raised $186.7 million.

item.title: Bank of England to Pay First Dividend to Treasury Since Pandemic
item.description: The Bank of England will resume paying a dividend to the UK government as it starts to bring its finances under control.

item.title: WEF Should Be Led by Person Without Corporate Links, Schwab Says
item.description: The World Economic Forum should be led by someone who doesn’t have a corporate background, according to its founder and former chairman Klaus Schwab, in an apparent dig at the organization’s current leadership.

item.title: Surveillance Firm Hawkeye 360 Jumps 30% After $416 Million IPO
item.description: Hawkeye 360 Inc. soared 30% after the provider of satellite-based signals intelligence for US government agencies raised $416 million in a US initial public offering priced at the top of a marketed range.
